steps.title: Identify the rhetorical move in the underlined sentence
steps.content: The first sentence frames Nixon primarily through the Watergate scandal, creating a one-dimensional perception. The underlined sentence begins with "But Nixon's legacy is complex:" signaling a direct contrast. It then presents Nixon's achievements and other aspects of his legacy — information that complicates and pushes back against the opening's narrow portrayal.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — It compares the achievements of two historical figures.: The underlined sentence centers on Nixon; its reference to the Barack Obama administration identifies when the Affordable Care Act became law rather than comparing Obama's achievements with Nixon's. ✗
  • Choice B — It offers information suggesting that a particular perception of a historical figure is an oversimplification.: The sentence counters the Watergate-only view of Nixon by presenting evidence of his broader legacy, showing that perception is incomplete. ✓
  • Choice C — It provides evidence supporting a claim about a historical figure and that figure's associates.: The underlined sentence focuses on Nixon's legacy; his associates are not discussed. ✗
  • Choice D — It explains why a historical figure's accomplishments are often disregarded by historians.: The sentence highlights Nixon's accomplishments but does not explain why historians disregard them. ✗
B is correct.
